cards.back: Dilated, tortuous superficial veins due to valve incompetence

cards.front: Which vein is most commonly affected in varicose veins?

cards.back: 4 cm below and lateral to the pubic tubercle
Test for SFJ incompetence: elevate leg → tourniquet at SFJ → stand → observe filling

cards.front: What is the gold standard investigation for varicose veins?

cards.back: Endovenous Laser Ablation — minimally invasive treatment for varicose veins

cards.front: What is the tap test (Schwartz test)?
cards.back: Tapping vein proximally transmits impulse distally if valves incompetent
Tests deep vein patency: tourniquet + walking → if pain/more prominent = DVT

cards.front: Where do venous ulcers typically occur?
What causes skin changes in chronic venous disease?
cards.back: Venous hypertension → capillary leakage → hemosiderin deposition, lipodermatosclerosis
Dilation at SFJ — has cough impulse, disappears on lying down
Compression stockings, leg elevation, weight loss, exercise
Injection of sclerosant foam to obliterate varicose veins
